name: Ella Logan
abstract: Ella Logan (born Georgina Armour Allan; 6 March 1913 – 1 May 1969) was a Scottish-American actress and singer who appeared on Broadway, recorded and had a nightclub career in the United States and internationally.

concerts.label: Anniversary of Pearl Harbor Program: "The Infamy of the Axis"
Children's Asthma Research Institute and Hospital 3rd Annual Parade of Stars

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: she was going to take a vacation and come to California with me, joining the theater with Paul Small and Finklehoff, Ella Logan's husband and an agent friend of his in "Curtain Time" and "Laugh Time" at the Current Theater in San Francisco.

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: My mother and father got an invitation from an aunt, who was a singer who was based in New York. Her name was Ella Logan. She was a wonderful performer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: Ella Logan was definitely in New York singing before I got here. And she appeared on some of the swing programs.

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't know who she was... Ella Logan and Lena were good friends and Ella Logan was Annie Ross’ aunt
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: So I was raised under the guardianship of an aunt, who was also a singer, called Ella Logan.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: I knew that her aunt was – oh, who was that woman? Ella, a musical comedy star. Ella Logan.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
